content:
# Recommendations — next plan iter (iter-032)

## Closest to completion — PRIORITIZE
1. **`pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_natural_extendScalarsCocycle`** (FBC, L2634) — the ONLY new
   open sorry; algebraic cocycle it consumes (`chartBaseChange_extendScalars_cocycle`) is ALREADY
   sorry-free. Remaining work is mechanical: (1) rewrite each of the 4 geometric legs via the
   **single-map** dictionary `pullbackSpecTildeNatIso φᵢ` / `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` — never compose
   two `Spec`-pullbacks, so `Γ(pullbackComp)` never whnf's; (2) both sides land among `tilde.functor`
   images, two routings differ by `chartBaseChange_extendScalars_cocycle`; (3) distribute `tilde` once
   via `Functor.map_comp`; close with the cocycle. **Dispatch a prover lane on this.**
   - WARNING (KB): the `Iso.ext`+`rw [chartBaseChangeGeometricComparison]` opening that the prover
     LSP-probed is exactly the `_concrete` route up to its kernel wall; the LSP HIDES the cold-bomb.
     The prover MUST keep each leg a single Spec-pullback and verify via cold `lake build`, not LSP.
2. **On success, retarget the crux** `pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_natural` body (currently
   `exact pullback_spec_tilde_iso_ring_square_mate_glue …`, the DEAD mate route) to the new
   realization. This is what finally eliminates a crux frontier sorry.

## Blocked / do-NOT-retry (no structural change first)
- **Mate / vComp / concrete / element routes** for the ring-square crux: ALL dead (mate-fold ×3,
  vComp carrier-whnf, element kernel-bomb, concrete `Γ(pullbackComp)` kernel wall, element route
  STRUCTURALLY dead by Mathlib gap). Do NOT re-assign `_mate_glue`, `_vcomp_glue`, `_catCommSq`,
  `_concrete`, `ring_square_cocycle`. The re-scoped cocycle route SUPERSEDES all of them.

## MEDIUM — refactor hygiene (lean-auditor iter031, recurring)
- **Excise compile-dead apparatus** once the crux retargets to `_extendScalarsCocycle` (sync `\uses`):
  `ring_square_cocycle` (L2017, refuted/unprovable statement), `_mate_glue` (L2041), `_vcomp_glue`
  (L2339), `_catCommSq` (L2385), `_concrete` (L2452), `base_change_mate_gstar_transpose` (L2785/L2918).
  `_mate_glue` is still LIVE (crux delegates to it) until step 2 above lands — excise AFTER.
- **5 no-op `set_option maxHeartbeats`** on `/-! -/` doc-headers (L2735, L2770–2773) — **5th
  consecutive iter unaddressed**. Inert but misleading; move to the guarded decls or delete. Cheap
  refactor — bundle with the excision pass.

## MEDIUM — blueprint prose reconciliation
- `lem:chartBaseChange_extendScalars_cocycle` prose claims the iso is built "natively … rather than an
  `eqToIso` transport" via cancelBaseChange/IsBaseChange. The landed Lean uses ONE `eqToIso` transport
  (same iso, sorry-free). Already `% NOTE`'d by this review. Planner: either soften the prose to match
  the eqToIso realization, or keep the cancelBaseChange version as a labelled "optional refinement."
  Not blocking — purely a prose/Lean honesty alignment.

## SNAP (no prover this iter — refactor staged for iter-032)
- The lax keystone + gate (iter-030) are LOAD-BEARING and landed. iter-032 = the
  localized-associator REFACTOR: redefine `tensorObjAssoc`/unitors/braiding as the structural isos of
  `modulesLocalizedMonoidal` transported via `tensorObjLocalizedIso` ⇒ `tensorPowAdd_*` pentagon FREE;
  re-route the four `sectionMul_*_core` via the landed gate + lax laws. This is a `refactor` subagent
  task (skeleton) + a separate prover fill — do NOT bundle into one budget (KB: re-base+re-prove gets
  timeout-killed).

## Coverage debt (`archon dag-query unmatched` = 91, unchanged)
Both new FBC decls ARE blueprinted. The 91 unmatched (no blueprint entry, invisible to the DAG) split:
- **~58 off-path SNAP internals**: `Scheme.Modules.RelativeTensorCoequalizer.*` (~22),
  `W_*` / `u*` / `objRestrict*` / `psh*` / monoidal-instance helpers. Slated for the SNAP off-path
  excision once the section/localized route closes — blueprinting now is throwaway.
- **~17 FBC-side helpers**: `base_change_mate_*` (dead apparatus), `pullbackSpecTilde{CatCommSq,
  VCompIso,VCompIso_hom_app}`, `chartBaseChange{GeometricComparison_eq_natApp,ModuleReassocNat,
  PushforwardNat,RestrictReassocNat}`, `conjugateIsoEquiv_{comp,symm_comp}`,
  `pullback_spec_tilde_iso_{hom,inv}_conjugateEquiv`, `*_concreteApply` helpers. The dead mate ones
  vanish in the excision pass; the real nat-transformers (`chartBaseChange*Nat`) deserve thin
  blueprint stubs — list for a blueprint-writer when the FBC chapter is next touched.

## Reusable proof pattern discovered
- **Algebra-level base-change cocycle via `extendScalarsComp` + one `eqToIso`**: to identify two
  bracketings of a triple `extendScalars` composite over a commuting ring square, fold each via
  `(ModuleCat.extendScalarsComp _ _).app M` and bridge by `eqToIso (by rw [hRing])` where `hRing` is
  the `CommRingCat.Hom.hom`-level square equality (`rw [← CommRingCat.hom_comp, …]; exact congrArg
  CommRingCat.Hom.hom <square>`). Stays entirely on `ModuleCat` carriers — no sheafification, no
  `Γ(pullbackComp)` whnf. This is the re-scope's load-bearing trick and it is light + cold-green.
